info.title: Nager.Date API Reference
info.description: The Nager.Date API is a free public service that provides reliable information about public holidays and long weekends for countries around the world. It allows developers to query national holiday dates, check whether a specific day is a public holiday, list upcoming holidays, and explore long-weekend data. By using the API, you agree to the Terms of Service. **Important note** > [!caution] > The API endpoints of version 4 are still in the beta phase and are subject to change. It is not yet advisable to use them for productive operation.

get.summary: Retrieve the list of all public holidays for the specified year and country
get.description:
This endpoint returns all officially recognized public holidays for the given country
and year. Each holiday entry includes the local and English holiday names, information
about whether the holiday applies nationally or only in specific subdivisions, and the
associated holiday type classifications.

parameters.name: year
parameters.description: The target year for which public holidays should be retrieved.

200.description: Successfully retrieved the list of public holidays.

400.description: The request was invalid. See the validation details for more information.

404.description: The provided country code is invalid or not recognized.

get.summary: Determines whether today is a public holiday in the specified country, optionally adjusted by a UTC offset
get.description:
By default, the calculation is based on the current UTC date.
You may optionally provide a timezone offset to evaluate the holiday status relative to a different local timezone.

This endpoint is optimized for simple command-line or automation workflows where only the HTTP status code is required

```
STATUSCODE=$(curl --silent --output /dev/stderr --write-out "%{http_code}" https://date.nager.at/Api/v3/IsTodayPublicHoliday/AT)
if [ $STATUSCODE -ne 200 ]; then
    # handle error
fi
```

parameters.name: subdivisionCode
parameters.description: Optional. The subdivision code (e.g., state, province) to narrow the check.

parameters.name: offset
parameters.description: Optional. UTC timezone offset in hours (range: -12 to +12).

204.description: Today is not a public holiday

400.description: The provided country code is invalid or not recognized.

get.summary: Retrieve all upcoming public holidays occurring within the next 365 days for a given country
get.description: The list includes only future holidays relative to the current date and is useful for forecasting, event planning, and applications that provide forward-looking holiday insights.

200.description: A list of upcoming public holidays for the next 365 days.

204.description: No upcoming holidays were found for the specified country.

get.summary: Retrieve all public holidays occurring worldwide within the next 7 days
get.description: This global endpoint aggregates upcoming holidays across all supported countries, enabling international systems to detect near-term events.

200.description: A collection of upcoming global public holidays.

get.summary: Retrieves the current version information of the Nager libraries.
get.description:
Returns detailed version information about the Nager implementation on the server,
including the exact NuGet package versions used by the API.

observedDate.description: The date on which the holiday is observed.

nationalHoliday.description: Indicates if this holiday applies to the entire country.

subdivisionCodes.description: ISO-3166-2 codes of the subdivisions where this holiday applies

holidayTypes.description: List of holiday types this holiday is classified under.

tags.name: PublicHoliday
tags.description: Includes functionality for retrieving public-holiday data, checking whether a specific day is a public holiday, and listing upcoming holidays. Supports both country-specific and worldwide holiday queries.

tags.name: Version
tags.description: Returns information about the current version of the Nager.Date API in use.
